chapter_title.en: Chapter on Delegating Hajj to Someone Who Undertakes it

text: 2870 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ لَيْسَ لَهُ مَالٌ حَجَّ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْ أَحَجَّهُ غَيْرُهُ ثُمَّ أَصَابَ مَالاً هَلْ عَلَيْهِ اَلْحَجُّ فَقَالَ «يُجْزِي عَنْهُمَا ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2870 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who did not have wealth and performed Hajj on behalf of another person or was sent for Hajj by someone else. Then, later, he acquired wealth. Is Hajj still obligatory upon him?
Imam (as) said: "It suffices for both."
